name: InSight
first: First seismometer on Mars; first interplanetary launch from US West Coast
description: InSight gave Mars its first stethoscope. Its seismometer, deployed by a robotic arm onto the regolith, recorded over 1,300 marsquakes — including a magnitude-5 in 2022, the largest ever detected on another planet. The data revealed Mars's interior structure: a 1,800-km-radius molten core, a thick crust, and surprising mantle layering. The mole heat-flow experiment failed when the regolith proved too cohesive for its self-hammering design. The mission ended when dust accumulation on solar panels reduced power below operational threshold.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Atlas V 401 from Vandenberg AFB — first interplanetary mission ever launched from the US West Coast.

events.label: EDL
events.note: Atmospheric entry, parachute, terminal-descent rocket landing at Elysium Planitia.

events.label: SEISMOMETER DEPLOYED
events.note: Robotic arm places SEIS instrument on the surface and shields it from wind.

events.label: MISSION END
events.note: Final low-power transmission 2022-12-15. Solar panels too dust-covered to recharge.
